Positive Contribution: Shows that the Gaussian variance of the cubic smearing diverges logarithmically. This provides the first genuine obstruction signal.
Boundary Information: Rules out bounded-variance scenarios in which the score function could exist.

Positive Contribution: Makes the Gaussian-to-interacting transfer question precise through a Cauchy-Schwarz bridge.
Boundary Information: Exposes the real bottleneck: a negative-moment bound is needed and is not established here.

Positive Contribution: Rewrites an analytic nonvanishing condition as a finite shell-isolation target.
Boundary Information: Rules out all multi-shell candidates and sharply narrows what a valid test vector must do.

Positive Contribution: Closes the rank-one base case through a concrete Gauss-sum calculation.
Boundary Information: Shows that the base-case mechanism is highly specific and does not by itself transfer to higher rank.

Positive Contribution: Demonstrates that shell isolation is achievable on a diagonal restriction.
Boundary Information: Leaves the central bridge question open: diagonal control is not yet full-integral control.

Positive Contribution: Invalid as stated; the characteristic polynomial kills the full sequence, including the base term.
Boundary Information: Eliminates recurrence-only shell isolation. Any successful operator must use initial-condition data, not just the recurrence.

Positive Contribution: The route does not produce a valid identification with the needed Fourier data.
Boundary Information: Rules out a tempting but structurally unsupported shortcut.

Positive Contribution: Constructs a stationary Markov chain for the target distribution.
Boundary Information: Does not touch the actual nontriviality constraint; it solves the easy local task but not the benchmark target.

Positive Contribution: Shows that the Metropolis-Hastings acceptance ratio uses the forbidden F^nu/F^mu dependence.
Boundary Information: Rules out the entire family of ratio-based acceptance rules as genuine solutions to this problem.

Positive Contribution: Poses a concrete candidate kernel and proves row-stochasticity under explicit assumptions.
Boundary Information: Leaves one crisp gap: invariance of the target measure is still unproved.

Positive Contribution: Finds additive invariants and closes the n=3 inequality through a convexity argument.
Boundary Information: Clarifies that the useful structure is genuinely low-dimensional and should not be naively lifted to all n.

Positive Contribution: An explicit counterexample shows that the semigroup or heat-flow route breaks real-rootedness.
Boundary Information: Removes an entire proof paradigm from consideration for the general case.

Positive Contribution: Verifies the quadratic equality case exactly.
Boundary Information: Adds no transferable mechanism beyond the degenerate one-parameter setting.

Positive Contribution: Connects equivariant cell data to nonequivariant information in geometric fixed points.
Boundary Information: Shows that any successful characterization must pass through this bridge rather than bypassing fixed-point data.

Positive Contribution: Invalid as written because it uses a monotonicity step that fails for incomplete transfer systems.
Boundary Information: Rules out the naive forward argument and isolates the missing monotone substitute.

Positive Contribution: Closes the reverse implication cleanly through localization plus fixed-point detection.
Boundary Information: Rules out counterexamples to the backward direction at the intended scope.

Positive Contribution: Gives the canonical variational characterization of the target notion.
Boundary Information: Eliminates alternative formulations that ignore the Schur complement.

Positive Contribution: One graph counterexample shows that raw edge-count conditions are insufficient.
Boundary Information: Permanently removes the raw cut-test family and justifies the need for effective conductance.

Positive Contribution: Provides an explicit lower bound on the effective cut functional using local data.
Boundary Information: Shows that the Schur-complement route is computationally tractable rather than purely existential.

Positive Contribution: Combines integrality from Q-acyclicity with index scaling to force a contradiction under an involution.
Boundary Information: Rules out a full structural class of candidate realizations, not just one example.

Positive Contribution: Turns an assumed input into a concrete literature dependency: a surface with the required Euler characteristic and even automorphism group must be located or proved to exist.
Boundary Information: Shows that the remaining gap is not in the obstruction logic above it, but in an external existence premise.

Positive Contribution: Computes a nonzero local Maslov index at the model vertex.
Boundary Information: Rules out local smoothability for this vertex type and provides a durable obstruction.

Positive Contribution: Combines the smooth-disk index calculation with the nonzero PL index to force contradiction.
Boundary Information: Shows that the obstruction is local and does not require global Hamiltonian machinery.

Positive Contribution: Proves that the core tensor is forced into the determinant orbit once all slice constraints are imposed.
Boundary Information: Rules out non-determinantal cores and explains why the remaining endgame becomes rigid.

Positive Contribution: Links the tensor condition to line-preserving incidence geometry and then invokes projective rigidity.
Boundary Information: Shows that the geometric bridge is not optional: any proof of core rigidity in this dimension must pass through the incidence structure.

Positive Contribution: Finishes the proof by showing that each block action collapses to a scalar.
Boundary Information: Rules out residual block-level freedom and forces rank-one scaling.

Positive Contribution: Gives an exact matrix-free matrix-vector product over the observation set with no O(N) operation.
Boundary Information: Rules out the need for explicit materialization of the full operator.

Positive Contribution: Introduces a separable surrogate whose inverse decomposes into tractable subproblems.
Boundary Information: Leaves open whether a sharper preconditioner exists for strongly nonuniform sampling.

Positive Contribution: Makes the remaining theoretical dependency explicit: concentration for without-replacement matrix sampling.
Boundary Information: Shows that the last gap is external theory rather than an algorithmic defect in the method itself.
